column3: Improper application of yarrow topically may lead to skin rashes, itching, or redness due to its potential allergenic properties.
Overconsumption of yarrow tea or extracts could result in gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea.
In some individuals, yarrow may trigger allergic responses, including skin irritation, difficulty breathing, or swelling.
Excessive intake of yarrow could interfere with blood clotting mechanisms, potentially increasing the risk of bleeding.
Prolonged exposure to sunlight after using yarrow oil topically might lead to increased sensitivity, causing skin reactions or sunburns.
Yarrow's hypotensive properties could lead to a sudden drop in blood pressure, causing dizziness or fainting.
Individuals with pre-existing conditions such as kidney disorders or allergies might experience worsened symptoms when using yarrow.
Yarrow could interact with certain medications, affecting their efficacy or intensifying side effects.
Prolonged or excessive use of yarrow might interfere with hormone levels, affecting menstrual cycles or hormonal balance.
Some individuals might experience drowsiness or dizziness as a side effect of yarrow, especially when consumed in large quantities or combined with other sedatives.
